title: Playwright Rendering, Isolation, Readiness, and Dynamic-DOM Evidence · Inference Brief: Rendered Provenance
summary: Every page launches a browser, shares cookies and storage, waits for network idle, captures unstable DOM, and leaves contexts or downloads alive. Predict authorization, URL identity, runtime admission, Promise ownership, resource bounds, accessibility impact, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler ts rendered provenance increment in isolated evidence-driven rendering adapter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Bind browser version, context policy, viewport, actions, DOM digest, screenshot or trace identity, console errors, and closure evidence.
Reapply origin, request type, redirect, download, WebSocket, worker, and SSRF rules to browser-initiated traffic.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Bind browser version, context policy, viewport, actions, DOM digest, screenshot or trace identity, console errors, and closure evidence.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Rendered Provenance model · Theory Rendered Prov…, name the owner, authorization, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, URL, robots, unknown value, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, browser, accessibility, report, handle, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Rendered Provenance precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Rendered Provenance model · Theory Rendered Prov…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 5254 during an authorized municipal service audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove necessity, context isolation, readiness, browser network policy, provenance, and closure; every Promise, stream, listener, timer, socket, file, browser context, and report sink needs one cleanup owner; then change one HTML repair, Cheerio mode, selector, unknown value, record schema, link relation, or metadata case and retain rejected evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Bind browser version, context policy, viewport, actions, DOM digest, screenshot or trace identity, console errors, and closure evidence.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Rendered Provenance model · Theory Rendered Prov…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Rendered HTML alone explains the browser state that produced it.

checks.description: The cumulative typescript portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker // Evidence: crawler-ts-rendered-provenance-1t4w43.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: crawler-ts-rendered-provenance-1t4w43(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?function\s+evidence_crawler_ts_rendered_provenance_1t4w43\s*\([^)]*\)\s*:\s*Readonly<\{[^}]+\}>\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?browserCases < 12)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?closedContexts < 12 \|\| closedContexts > browserCases)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?readinessChanges < 5)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?policyEscapes > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
